# never-allow.txt — THIRD, UNCONDITIONAL gate. Independent of allowlist.txt.
#
# Rationale (2026-07-16 sanitizer audit, requested after the PR #6 incident
# review): the audit found sanitize.sh + allowlist.txt were NOT regressed —
# every one of the 6 dispatches that day correctly stripped .gemini/ and
# scripts/ from the sanitized snapshot (see PR #6's diff: 291 .gemini/ + 29
# scripts/ entries are all `removed`, none `added` — the pipeline was about
# to DELETE a pre-existing, unrelated public-main exposure from 2026-07-05,
# not create a new one). No code bug existed.
#
# But the near-miss is real: allowlist.txt is a single human-edited file. A
# future PR could add ".gemini" or "scripts" to it by mistake (fat-finger,
# bad merge, stale copy-paste) and the PRIMARY gate would silently accept it
# — same class of bug the 2026-07-15 real-tree audit caught 4x under the old
# denylist model. This file is a second, independently-maintained list that
# sanitize.sh checks UNCONDITIONALLY after the allowlist+denylist passes,
# regardless of what allowlist.txt says and regardless of STRICT_ALLOWLIST.
# To ever let one of these paths into a public snapshot, a human must edit
# BOTH allowlist.txt AND this file — a single mis-edit can no longer leak.
#
# One top-level name per line. Adding an entry here always wins over
# allowlist.txt. Removing an entry requires the same per-path human review
# standard as promoting something in allowlist.txt.

.gemini
scripts
pydantic-agents
packages
huashu-design
infra
integrations
skills
secrets
credentials
.planning
